risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: “Two randomisation lists are computer generated by a statistician otherwise not involved in the study. The lists are stored by the two regional study coordinators.” Comment: This describes a proper random allocation sequence generated by an independent statistician using a computer-generated method, which is sufficient for Low risk under Cochrane guidance.

_evidence.method_quote: “Two randomisation lists are computer generated by a statistician otherwise not involved in the study. The lists are stored by the two regional study coordinators. After the definitive inclusion of a general practice into the study, the coordinator of the respective region calls the coordinator of the other region who, after registering the practice, hands out the allocation information.”
_evidence.method_summary: Cluster random allocation lists were computer-generated by an independent statistician and stored by regional coordinators.
context_clues: controlled multicenter prospective study with cluster random allocation
by a statistician otherwise not involved in the study
stored by the two regional study coordinators

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Two randomisation lists are computer generated by a statistician otherwise not involved in the study. The lists are stored by the two regional study coordinators. After the definitive inclusion of a general practice into the study, the coordinator of the respective region calls the coordinator of the other region who, after registering the practice, hands out the allocation information." Comment: This describes a central allocation procedure handled by coordinators rather than the recruiter, which prevents foreknowledge of the upcoming assignment before enrollment of each practice.

_evidence.concealment_quote: Two randomisation lists are computer generated by a statistician otherwise not involved in the study. The lists are stored by the two regional study coordinators. After the definitive inclusion of a general practice into the study, the coordinator of the respective region calls the coordinator of the other region who, after registering the practice, hands out the allocation information.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The study design takes into account the principles set out in the Helsinki declaration (Seoul, 2008). All patients have to sign informed consent forms." Comment: The article does not state whether participants or the personnel delivering the intervention were blinded, and it gives no blinding method. Because blinding status is not described, and although the study includes subjective outcomes (e.g. fear of falling, quality of life, physical activity), there is insufficient explicit evidence to judge performance bias as low or high.

_evidence.blinding_quote: "The study design takes into account the principles set out in the Helsinki declaration (Seoul, 2008). All patients have to sign informed consent forms."
outcome_categories: incidence and frequency of fall-related injuries
objective_outcomes: incidence and frequency of fall-related injuries

risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: Outcomes were assessed by a mix of patient self-report questionnaires/diaries and structured telephone interviews, and the extracted evidence indicates the assessor was not blinded for some patient-reported outcomes: "number of falls" (patient-reported, assessor blinded: no) and "fall-related injuries" (patient-reported, assessor blinded: no). The study also reports subjective outcomes including "Fear of Falling (FES-I)", "EuroQol quality of life", and "physical activity (PAR)". Comment: Because the patient is the assessor for self-reported outcomes, and at least one subjective outcome was unblinded, knowledge of allocation could bias outcome measurement; under the worst-outcome rule this is High risk even though some balance tests are more objective.

outcomes.label: risk of fall assessment (Timed Up and Go, Chair Rising test, Romberg-Test, Tandem and Semi-Tandem Stand)

risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The provided text states that analyses will use the "full-analysis set" and follow the "intention to treat principle," but it does not report actual post-randomization attrition numbers for the analysed outcomes (no CONSORT flow, no per-arm randomized vs analysed counts, no reasons for loss at the analysed time point). Comment: Without observed attrition data, the extent and balance of missing outcome data cannot be judged, so this domain remains unclear rather than high or low risk.

_evidence.notes: No CONSORT-style flow or per-arm analysed numbers are reported in the provided text. Methods state: "Analyses of the primary endpoint... will be performed on base of the full-analysis set" and "intention to treat principle."
